caption: A presentation slide for a course on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" is shown, specifically for "Tutorial – 1". The slide features a mathematical formula for $y$, a table of thermodynamic properties including pressure "p (bar)", temperature "T (K)", enthalpy "h (j/g)", and entropy "s (j/gK)" for states 1, 2, f, and g. A specific constant, $\varepsilon_2 = 0.95$, is noted. Below the table, a calculation for $y_2$ is displayed, substituting values from the table into the formula to yield the result "25.466 / 423.1". The bottom of the slide credits "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay" and includes the "NPTEL" logo. As the sequence progresses, a final result of "= 0.060" is written in red text next to the calculation. This red text appears first on the right side of the equation and then moves to the left side in subsequent frames.
